1	// Package try — Attempt is the C2 shell that wraps the existing
2	// agent.ExecuteBeadWithConfig + agent.LandBeadResult + (optional)
3	// agent.DefaultBeadReviewer pipeline and returns a try.Outcome via the C1
4	// ToOutcome adapter. It has no production callers at end of C2; the
5	// orchestrator continues to drive the legacy executor directly. Callers
6	// migrate in C3+.
7	package try
8	
9	import (
10		"context"
11	
12		"github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/agent"
13		"github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/config"
14	)
15	
16	// AttemptOpts bundles the knobs Attempt needs to drive the legacy
17	// ExecuteBeadWithConfig + LandBeadResult + DefaultBeadReviewer pipeline.
18	//
19	// Tests inject Executor to supply a canned ExecuteBeadReport without
20	// touching git or a real harness. When Executor is non-nil it short-circuits
21	// the legacy pipeline entirely; the remaining fields are ignored.
22	type AttemptOpts struct {
23		// Executor is an optional injection seam. When non-nil, Attempt delegates
24		// to it instead of calling ExecuteBeadWithConfig + LandBeadResult.
25		Executor agent.ExecuteBeadExecutor
26	
27		// ProjectRoot is the repo the bead executes in.
28		ProjectRoot string
29	
30		// Rcfg is the resolved harness/model/profile config for the attempt.
31		Rcfg config.ResolvedConfig
32	
33		// Runtime carries non-durable injection seams (BeadEvents, AgentRunner, …).
34		Runtime agent.ExecuteBeadRuntime
35	
36		// GitOps is the worker-side git surface (worktree add/remove, head, …).
37		GitOps agent.GitOps
38	
39		// OrchestratorGitOps is the parent-side git surface (UpdateRef).
40		OrchestratorGitOps agent.OrchestratorGitOps
41	
42		// LandingOpts controls how LandBeadResult lands a completed worker result.
43		LandingOpts agent.BeadLandingOptions
44	
45		// Reviewer, when non-nil, is invoked after a successful merge to validate
46		// the commit against the bead's AC. When nil, post-merge review is skipped.
47		Reviewer agent.BeadReviewer
48	}
49	
50	// Attempt runs one bead attempt and returns a try.Outcome.
51	//
52	// When opts.Executor is non-nil, Attempt delegates to it and converts the
53	// returned ExecuteBeadReport via ToOutcome — the path covered by
54	// TestAttempt_WrapsLegacyExecutor.
55	//
56	// Otherwise Attempt drives the legacy pipeline:
57	//
58	//  1. agent.ExecuteBeadWithConfig — run the agent in an isolated worktree.
59	//  2. agent.LandBeadResult — merge or preserve, evaluate gates.
60	//  3. opts.Reviewer.ReviewBead (best effort) — optional post-merge review.
61	//
62	// The store argument is reserved for the C3+ migration where Attempt will
63	// own bead-tracker bookkeeping (claim, heartbeat, close). At C2 it is
64	// accepted but not yet used; the legacy loop still owns those calls.
65	func Attempt(ctx context.Context, store agent.ExecuteBeadLoopStore, beadID string, opts AttemptOpts) (Outcome, error) {
66		_ = store // reserved for C3+ migration; legacy loop still owns store I/O.
67	
68		if opts.Executor != nil {
69			report, err := opts.Executor.Execute(ctx, beadID)
70			if err != nil {
71				out := ToOutcome(report)
72				if out.BeadID == "" {
73					out.BeadID = beadID
74				}
75				return out, err
76			}
77			out := ToOutcome(report)
78			if out.BeadID == "" {
79				out.BeadID = beadID
80			}
81			return out, nil
82		}
83	
84		res, execErr := agent.ExecuteBeadWithConfig(ctx, opts.ProjectRoot, beadID, opts.Rcfg, opts.Runtime, opts.GitOps)
85		if execErr != nil && res == nil {
86			return Outcome{BeadID: beadID}, execErr
87		}
88	
89		if res != nil {
90			landing, landErr := agent.LandBeadResult(opts.ProjectRoot, res, opts.OrchestratorGitOps, opts.LandingOpts)
91			if landErr == nil {
92				agent.ApplyLandingToResult(res, landing)
93			} else if execErr == nil {
94				execErr = landErr
95			}
96		}
97	
98		if opts.Reviewer != nil && res != nil && res.ResultRev != "" && res.ResultRev != res.BaseRev && res.ExitCode == 0 {
99			// Best-effort review at C2; the loop's review state machine is what
100			// production callers continue to drive. C3+ moves that here.
101			_, _ = opts.Reviewer.ReviewBead(ctx, beadID, res.ResultRev, agent.ImplementerRouting{
102				Harness:     res.Harness,
103				Provider:    res.Provider,
104				Model:       res.Model,
105				ActualPower: res.ActualPower,
106			})
107		}
108	
109		report := reportFromResult(res)
110		out := ToOutcome(report)
111		if out.BeadID == "" {
112			out.BeadID = beadID
113		}
114		return out, execErr
115	}
116	
117	// reportFromResult projects the subset of ExecuteBeadResult fields that
118	// ToOutcome consumes. It is intentionally narrow — the loop's full report
119	// synthesis (tier, profile telemetry, escalation count) is not needed for
120	// the C2 shell and gets built out as callers migrate in C3+.
121	func reportFromResult(res *agent.ExecuteBeadResult) agent.ExecuteBeadReport {
122		if res == nil {
123			return agent.ExecuteBeadReport{}
124		}
125		return agent.ExecuteBeadReport{
126			BeadID:             res.BeadID,
127			AttemptID:          res.AttemptID,
128			WorkerID:           res.WorkerID,
129			Harness:            res.Harness,
130			Provider:           res.Provider,
131			Model:              res.Model,
132			ActualPower:        res.ActualPower,
133			Status:             res.Status,
134			Detail:             res.Detail,
135			SessionID:          res.SessionID,
136			BaseRev:            res.BaseRev,
137			ResultRev:          res.ResultRev,
138			PreserveRef:        res.PreserveRef,
139			NoChangesRationale: res.NoChangesRationale,
140			CostUSD:            res.CostUSD,
141			DurationMS:         int64(res.DurationMS),
142		}
143	}

1	// Package try defines the sealed Outcome sum type that the execute-bead
2	// orchestrator will return once the C-series refactor lands. The types in
3	// this file are introduced ahead of any caller (see ddx-240f2082) so that
4	// later beads can migrate the orchestrator to produce Outcome values
5	// without simultaneously inventing the vocabulary.
6	package try
7	
8	import (
9		"fmt"
10		"time"
11	
12		"github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/agent"
13		"github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/bead"
14	)
15	
16	// Disposition is the closed set of post-attempt routing decisions the
17	// execute-loop can take on a bead. Every Outcome carries exactly one.
18	type Disposition int
19	
20	const (
21		DispositionMerged Disposition = iota
22		DispositionAlreadyDone
23		DispositionRetry
24		DispositionPark
25		DispositionNeedsHuman
26		DispositionLoopError
27	)
28	
29	func (d Disposition) String() string {
30		switch d {
31		case DispositionMerged:
32			return "merged"
33		case DispositionAlreadyDone:
34			return "already_done"
35		case DispositionRetry:
36			return "retry"
37		case DispositionPark:
38			return "park"
39		case DispositionNeedsHuman:
40			return "needs_human"
41		case DispositionLoopError:
42			return "loop_error"
43		default:
44			return fmt.Sprintf("disposition(%d)", int(d))
45		}
46	}
47	
48	// ParkReason enumerates the closed vocabulary of reasons the loop may park
49	// a bead. String-typed for ergonomic comparison, but every value used in
50	// production must come from the constants below; ParkReasonValid enforces
51	// that at the boundary.
52	type ParkReason string
53	
54	const (
55		ParkReasonNeedsReview              ParkReason = "needs_review"
56		ParkReasonDecomposition            ParkReason = "decomposition"
57		ParkReasonPushFailed               ParkReason = "push_failed"
58		ParkReasonPushConflict             ParkReason = "push_conflict"
59		ParkReasonCostCap                  ParkReason = "cost_cap"
60		ParkReasonLoopError                ParkReason = "loop_error"
61		ParkReasonNoChangesUnverified      ParkReason = "no_changes_unverified"
62		ParkReasonNoChangesUnjustified     ParkReason = "no_changes_unjustified"
63		ParkReasonRateLimitBudgetExhausted ParkReason = "rate_limit_budget_exhausted"
64		ParkReasonQuotaPaused              ParkReason = "quota_paused"
65		ParkReasonLockContention           ParkReason = "lock_contention"
66	)
67	
68	// AllParkReasons returns the canonical list of ParkReason values in a
69	// stable order. Used by tests and by callers that need to enumerate the
70	// vocabulary (e.g. for documentation generators).
71	func AllParkReasons() []ParkReason {
72		return []ParkReason{
73			ParkReasonNeedsReview,
74			ParkReasonDecomposition,
75			ParkReasonPushFailed,
76			ParkReasonPushConflict,
77			ParkReasonCostCap,
78			ParkReasonLoopError,
79			ParkReasonNoChangesUnverified,
80			ParkReasonNoChangesUnjustified,
81			ParkReasonRateLimitBudgetExhausted,
82			ParkReasonQuotaPaused,
83			ParkReasonLockContention,
84		}
85	}
86	
87	// ParkReasonValid reports whether r is a member of the closed ParkReason
88	// vocabulary. The empty string is invalid; use a zero ParkReason only on
89	// non-Park dispositions.
90	func ParkReasonValid(r ParkReason) bool {
91		for _, p := range AllParkReasons() {
92			if p == r {
93				return true
94			}
95		}
96		return false
97	}
98	
99	func (p ParkReason) String() string { return string(p) }
100	
101	// Outcome is the sealed sum type the execute-loop orchestrator will return
102	// for each bead attempt. It carries the routing decision (Disposition)
103	// along with the small set of facts the loop needs to apply that decision:
104	// timing, cost, the resulting commit (if any), and the bead-tracker events
105	// to record.
106	type Outcome struct {
107		BeadID       string
108		AttemptID    string
109		BaseRev      string
110		ResultRev    string
111		SessionID    string
112		DurationMS   int64
113		CostUSD      float64
114		Disposition  Disposition
115		Cooldown     time.Duration
116		ParkReason   ParkReason
117		RecordEvents []bead.BeadEvent
118	}
119	
120	// dispositionToStatus maps a (Disposition, ParkReason) pair onto the
121	// canonical execute-bead status string. The mapping is deterministic and
122	// invertible for the canonical reports produced by FromOutcome.
123	func dispositionToStatus(d Disposition, p ParkReason) string {
124		switch d {
125		case DispositionMerged:
126			return agent.ExecuteBeadStatusSuccess
127		case DispositionAlreadyDone:
128			return agent.ExecuteBeadStatusAlreadySatisfied
129		case DispositionRetry:
130			return agent.ExecuteBeadStatusNoChanges
131		case DispositionNeedsHuman:
132			return agent.ExecuteBeadStatusLandConflictNeedsHuman
133		case DispositionLoopError:
134			return agent.ExecuteBeadStatusExecutionFailed
135		case DispositionPark:
136			switch p {
137			case ParkReasonNeedsReview:
138				return agent.ExecuteBeadStatusPreservedNeedsReview
139			case ParkReasonDecomposition:
140				return agent.ExecuteBeadStatusDeclinedNeedsDecomposition
141			case ParkReasonPushFailed:
142				return agent.ExecuteBeadStatusPushFailed
143			case ParkReasonPushConflict:
144				return agent.ExecuteBeadStatusPushConflict
145			case ParkReasonNoChangesUnverified, ParkReasonNoChangesUnjustified:
146				return agent.ExecuteBeadStatusNoChanges
147			default:
148				// Park reasons without a dedicated status share execution_failed
149				// and rely on Detail (= ParkReason) to disambiguate on inverse.
150				return agent.ExecuteBeadStatusExecutionFailed
151			}
152		}
153		return ""
154	}
155	
156	// FromOutcome produces the canonical ExecuteBeadReport that ToOutcome
157	// inverts. RecordEvents are not carried (ExecuteBeadReport has no events
158	// field); callers that need events handle them out-of-band.
159	func FromOutcome(o Outcome) agent.ExecuteBeadReport {
160		rep := agent.ExecuteBeadReport{
161			BeadID:     o.BeadID,
162			AttemptID:  o.AttemptID,
163			Status:     dispositionToStatus(o.Disposition, o.ParkReason),
164			SessionID:  o.SessionID,
165			BaseRev:    o.BaseRev,
166			ResultRev:  o.ResultRev,
167			CostUSD:    o.CostUSD,
168			DurationMS: o.DurationMS,
169		}
170		if o.Cooldown > 0 {
171			rep.RetryAfter = o.Cooldown.String()
172		}
173		if o.Disposition == DispositionPark && o.ParkReason != "" {
174			rep.Detail = string(o.ParkReason)
175		}
176		return rep
177	}
178	
179	// ToOutcome decodes an ExecuteBeadReport back into an Outcome. It is the
180	// inverse of FromOutcome on canonical reports; reports produced by other
181	// code paths may carry fields Outcome does not model, which are dropped.
182	func ToOutcome(r agent.ExecuteBeadReport) Outcome {
183		o := Outcome{
184			BeadID:     r.BeadID,
185			AttemptID:  r.AttemptID,
186			BaseRev:    r.BaseRev,
187			ResultRev:  r.ResultRev,
188			SessionID:  r.SessionID,
189			DurationMS: r.DurationMS,
190			CostUSD:    r.CostUSD,
191		}
192		if r.RetryAfter != "" {
193			if d, err := time.ParseDuration(r.RetryAfter); err == nil {
194				o.Cooldown = d
195			}
196		}
197		switch r.Status {
198		case agent.ExecuteBeadStatusSuccess:
199			o.Disposition = DispositionMerged
200		case agent.ExecuteBeadStatusAlreadySatisfied:
201			o.Disposition = DispositionAlreadyDone
202		case agent.ExecuteBeadStatusNoChanges:
203			// Detail carrying a valid ParkReason promotes to Park; otherwise Retry.
204			if pr := ParkReason(r.Detail); ParkReasonValid(pr) &&
205				(pr == ParkReasonNoChangesUnverified || pr == ParkReasonNoChangesUnjustified) {
206				o.Disposition = DispositionPark
207				o.ParkReason = pr
208			} else {
209				o.Disposition = DispositionRetry
210			}
211		case agent.ExecuteBeadStatusPreservedNeedsReview:
212			o.Disposition = DispositionPark
213			o.ParkReason = ParkReasonNeedsReview
214		case agent.ExecuteBeadStatusDeclinedNeedsDecomposition:
215			o.Disposition = DispositionPark
216			o.ParkReason = ParkReasonDecomposition
217		case agent.ExecuteBeadStatusPushFailed:
218			o.Disposition = DispositionPark
219			o.ParkReason = ParkReasonPushFailed
220		case agent.ExecuteBeadStatusPushConflict:
221			o.Disposition = DispositionPark
222			o.ParkReason = ParkReasonPushConflict
223		case agent.ExecuteBeadStatusLandConflictNeedsHuman:
224			o.Disposition = DispositionNeedsHuman
225		case agent.ExecuteBeadStatusExecutionFailed:
226			// Detail carrying a valid ParkReason disambiguates Park from LoopError.
227			if pr := ParkReason(r.Detail); ParkReasonValid(pr) {
228				o.Disposition = DispositionPark
229				o.ParkReason = pr
230			} else {
231				o.Disposition = DispositionLoopError
232			}
233		}
234		return o
235	}

13	// TriageTierHintKey is the bead Extra key that records the triage policy's
14	// tier-pin hint for the next attempt. Populated when the BLOCK ladder reaches
15	// the escalate_tier rung; consumed by tier-aware executors to start above the
16	// default tier on the next drain pass.
17	const TriageTierHintKey = "triage.tier_hint"
18	
19	// TriageNeedsHumanLabel is appended to a bead when the BLOCK ladder reaches
20	// its terminal rung. The execute-loop label-filter and the human triage UI
21	// both use this label to surface beads that no longer benefit from automated
22	// re-attempts.
23	const TriageNeedsHumanLabel = "needs_human"
24	
25	// triageDecisionEventKind is the event kind used to record the triage
26	// policy's chosen action after a non-APPROVE review verdict.
27	const triageDecisionEventKind = "triage-decision"
28	
29	// applyReviewTriageDecision runs the triage policy after a BLOCK or
30	// REQUEST_CHANGES review verdict has been recorded for `beadID`. It reads
31	// prior review events to compute the BLOCK count, biases the result toward
32	// re-attempt when the latest BLOCK was paired with a degraded reviewer
33	// (kind:review-pairing-degraded), then applies the chosen action by writing
34	// metadata, labels, and a kind:triage-decision event.
35	//
36	// Errors from the underlying store are best-effort: the loop continues
37	// regardless so a triage-side failure cannot strand a reopened bead.
38	func (w *ExecuteBeadWorker) applyReviewTriageDecision(beadID, actor string, now time.Time, currentTier string) error {
39		events, err := w.Store.Events(beadID)
40		if err != nil {
41			return err
42		}
43	
44		var blockTimestamps []time.Time
45		var pairingDegraded []time.Time
46		for _, ev := range events {
47			switch {
48			case ev.Kind == "review" && strings.TrimSpace(ev.Summary) == "BLOCK":
49				blockTimestamps = append(blockTimestamps, ev.CreatedAt)
50			case ev.Kind == ReviewPairingDegradedEventKind:

52			}
53		}
54

56		// is the last entry in blockTimestamps and must not be counted as prior
57		// history per triage.Decide()'s contract.
58		historyCount := len(blockTimestamps)
59		if historyCount > 0 {

61		}
62		history := make([]triage.FailureMode, 0, historyCount)
63		for i := 0; i < historyCount; i++ {

65		}
66

68		action := policy.Decide(beadID, triage.FailureModeReviewBlock, history)
69	
70		pairedDegraded := latestBlockPairedDegraded(blockTimestamps, pairingDegraded)
71		if pairedDegraded && action == triage.ActionEscalateTier {
72			action = triage.ActionReAttemptWithContext
73		}
74	
75		return w.applyTriageAction(beadID, actor, now, action, currentTier, pairedDegraded)
76	}
77	
78	// latestBlockPairedDegraded reports whether the most recent BLOCK event was
79	// paired with a review-pairing-degraded event from the same attempt window
80	// (between the previous BLOCK and this one).
81	func latestBlockPairedDegraded(blocks, pairing []time.Time) bool {
82		if len(blocks) == 0 || len(pairing) == 0 {
83			return false
84		}
85		latest := blocks[len(blocks)-1]
86		var prev time.Time
87		if len(blocks) >= 2 {
88			prev = blocks[len(blocks)-2]
89		}
90		for _, t := range pairing {
91			if t.After(prev) && !t.After(latest) {
92				return true
93			}
94		}
95		return false
96	}
97	
98	// applyTriageAction performs the side effects for a chosen Action: writes a
99	// tier-pin hint into bead.Extra (escalate_tier), appends a needs_human label
100	// (needs_human), and always records a kind:triage-decision event.
101	func (w *ExecuteBeadWorker) applyTriageAction(beadID, actor string, now time.Time, action triage.Action, currentTier string, pairedDegraded bool) error {
102		body := map[string]any{
103			"action": string(action),
104			"mode":   string(triage.FailureModeReviewBlock),
105		}
106		if pairedDegraded {
107			body["pairing_degraded"] = true
108		}
109	
110		switch action {
111		case triage.ActionEscalateTier:
112			nextTier := nextEscalatedTier(currentTier)
113			body["tier_hint"] = string(nextTier)
114			_ = w.Store.Update(beadID, func(b *bead.Bead) {
115				if b.Extra == nil {
116					b.Extra = make(map[string]any)
117				}
118				b.Extra[TriageTierHintKey] = string(nextTier)
119			})
120		case triage.ActionNeedsHuman:
121			_ = w.Store.Update(beadID, func(b *bead.Bead) {
122				if !HasBeadLabel(b.Labels, TriageNeedsHumanLabel) {
123					b.Labels = append(b.Labels, TriageNeedsHumanLabel)
124				}
125			})
126		}
127	
128		bodyJSON, _ := json.Marshal(body)
129		return w.Store.AppendEvent(beadID, bead.BeadEvent{
130			Kind:      triageDecisionEventKind,
131			Summary:   string(triage.FailureModeReviewBlock) + ": " + string(action),
132			Body:      string(bodyJSON),
133			Actor:     actor,
134			Source:    "ddx agent execute-loop",
135			CreatedAt: now,
136		})
137	}
138	
139	// nextEscalatedTier returns the next tier above `current`. An unrecognised or
140	// empty current tier defaults to standard; smart is its own ceiling.
141	func nextEscalatedTier(current string) escalation.ModelTier {
142		switch escalation.ModelTier(current) {
143		case escalation.TierCheap:
144			return escalation.TierStandard
145		case escalation.TierStandard:
146			return escalation.TierSmart
147		case escalation.TierSmart:
148			return escalation.TierSmart
149		default:
150			return escalation.TierStandard
151		}
152	}

`cli/internal/agent/runner.go:239` (Runner.Run) and `cli/internal/agent/agent_runner.go:163` (Runner.RunAgent) both call `context.WithCancel(context.Background())`, discarding any context from the caller. `RunOptions` exposes no ctx field. `agent.ExecuteBead` likewise takes no ctx, so `server.WorkerManager.runWorker`'s ctx never reaches the agent call.

As a result, `WorkerManager.Stop(id)` (server/workers.go:648) cancels only the worker goroutine; the embedded `agentlib.Run` call (and any subprocess it launched) keeps running until SIGKILL. This is the structural defect behind 33–142h hung workers.

## Scope

1. Add `Context context.Context` to `RunOptions` (or change signatures to `Run(ctx, opts)`). Default to `context.Background()` when nil.
2. Derive the internal ctx from the caller's rather than `context.Background()`.
3. Thread ctx through `agent.ExecuteBead` → `runner.Run` → `agentlib.Run` → provider HTTP.
4. Update `server.WorkerManager.runWorker`'s `singleTierAttempt` closure to pass `ctx` into `agent.ExecuteBead`.

## Non-goals

- New wall-clock timeout logic (covered by sibling RC2 bead)
- Operator-facing stop command (covered by ddx-b808df39)

The `DefaultTimeoutMS = 7200000` (2h) in `cli/internal/agent/types.go:363` is advertised as a hard cap but implemented as an *idle timeout*:

- `cli/internal/agent/executor.go:154-178` resets `time.NewTimer(idleTimeout)` on every stdout/stderr byte.
- `cli/internal/agent/agent_runner.go:264-285` resets on every non-compaction agentlib event.

A provider that emits any heartbeat, retry line, or periodic event defeats the timer indefinitely — enabling the 33–142h hangs observed on agent projects.

Only the claude streaming path (`cli/internal/agent/claude_stream.go:373-384`) uses a true wall-clock deadline via `time.After(timeout)`. That path is why bundle `20260415T015231-c2ffa9ba` fired at exactly 2h 0m 0.098s — and nothing else does.

## Scope

1. Add an absolute wall-clock deadline in addition to (not replacing) the idle timeout.
2. Default the wall-clock bound to something operators can trust — e.g. 3x `DefaultTimeoutMS` or a new `DefaultWallClockMS`.
3. Apply in: `OSExecutor.ExecuteInDir` (subprocess path) and `Runner.RunAgent` (embedded agent path).
4. Reflect the wall-clock error in result.json as a distinct `error: "wall-clock deadline exceeded after Xh"` so it is not confused with idle timeout.

## Non-goals

- Lowering or removing the existing idle timeout.
- Per-provider tuning (can be follow-up).","events_attachment":".execute-bead-wt-ddx-0a651925-20260418T043148-1346e8a3-b203c889/events.jsonl","execute-loop-heartbeat-at":"2026-04-18T05:29:25.624987414Z","id":".execute-bead-wt-ddx-0a651925-20260418T043148-1346e8a3-b203c889","issue_type":"task","labels":["ddx","phase:build","kind:bug","area:agent","area:workers","root-cause:rc2"],"owner":"erik","parent":"ddx-0a651925","priority":0,"session_id":"eb-0acc00fd","status":"closed","title":"execute-bead: add wall-clock deadline alongside idle timeout for agent harness","updated_at":"2026-04-18T05:30:49.793036993Z"}

Routing currently does not check whether the resolved model's context window can hold the bead prompt. A 32k-context fuzzy match against an 80k-token bead prompt routes 'successfully' and fails mid-dispatch with a provider 400 or truncated output.

## Scope

- Add optional EstimatedPromptTokens (uint32) to RouteRequest (cli/internal/agent/types.go).
- In evaluateCandidate (cli/internal/agent/routing.go), when EstimatedPromptTokens is set and > 0, reject (or penalize) candidates whose concrete_model context window is below it.
- Source of context window per candidate: v0.3.11's LookupModelLimits first, then catalog ModelEntry.ContextWindow (cli/internal/agent/catalog.go) as fallback. If neither is known, treat as 'unknown' — do NOT reject (avoid false negatives), but log at -v.
- Caller side: pass EstimatedPromptTokens from execute-bead when the bead prompt is assembled (best-effort — can use char/4 heuristic if a tokenizer is unavailable).

## Out of scope

- Tool-calling, structured-output, effort, permissions gating

18		last := base

19	// per-invocation runtime intent for an execute-bead loop run. Durable knobs
20	// (assignee, retry caps, harness/model, tier bounds, etc.) live on
21	// config.ResolvedConfig and are passed via Run's rcfg argument.

23		Log          io.Writer
24		EventSink    io.Writer

26		PreClaimHook func(ctx context.Context) error
27		// RoutePreflight, when non-nil, is invoked between nextCandidate and
28		// Claim. It is expected to call upstream ResolveRoute against the
29		// loop's resolved (harness, model) and return whatever typed routing
30		// error the upstream surfaced — notably agent.ErrHarnessModelIncompatible
31		// when the harness allow-list rejects the model. Any non-nil error
32		// causes the loop to exit immediately without claiming the bead, with
33		// a worker-level execution_failed record naming the rejected pair.
34		// DDx does NOT duplicate the upstream allow-list; this gate only
35		// consumes the typed-incompatibility surface.
36		RoutePreflight func(ctx context.Context, harness, model string) error
37		Once           bool

45		// so the loop re-scans the queue. Used by the operator-prompt approve /
46		// auto-approve mutations (Story 15) to avoid a poll-interval-sized delay
47		// before a freshly-approved bead is claimed. Implementations must send
48		// non-blocking (server-side helpers do); the loop only waits for a
49		// receive on WakeCh during the idle sleep, never elsewhere.
50		WakeCh <-chan struct{}
51	}

53	// DefaultReviewMaxRetries is the number of reviewer attempts allowed per
54	// committed result_rev before the loop emits a terminal
55	// `review-manual-required` event and stops re-executing primary. FEAT-022 §14.
56	const DefaultReviewMaxRetries = 3
57	
58	// MaxLoopCooldown is the absolute upper bound the execute-loop will set for
59	// any execute-loop-retry-after value. Year-scale parks effectively mean
60	// "never retry" and that should be a deliberate operator choice via
61	// `ddx bead update --set execute-loop-retry-after=...`, not an automatic
62	// loop decision. Beyond this cap, the loop refuses to lengthen the cooldown
63	// further; an operator extending it manually still works.
64	const MaxLoopCooldown = 24 * time.Hour
65	
66	// LandConflictCooldown is the short cooldown applied when all conflict-recovery
67	// paths fail (3-way ort auto-merge + optional focused-resolve agent) and the
68	// bead is parked as land_conflict_unresolvable. Much shorter than
69	// MaxLoopCooldown because land conflicts often resolve quickly as sibling beads
70	// advance the base branch.
71	const LandConflictCooldown = 15 * time.Minute
72	
73	// StoreErrorCooldown is the short cooldown applied when a Store.* operation in
74	// the outcome-handling block returns a transient error. It prevents an
75	// immediate re-queue of the affected bead while allowing the loop to continue
76	// to the next candidate. Store errors during outcome handling are non-fatal:
77	// they are logged and emitted as loop-error events but do not terminate the
78	// worker.
79	const StoreErrorCooldown = 5 * time.Minute
80	
81	// handleOutcomeStoreError is called when a Store.* operation in the
82	// post-Execute outcome block returns a non-nil error. It logs the error,
83	// appends a kind:loop-error event to the bead (best-effort), sets a short
84	// cooldown so the bead is not immediately re-queued, increments
85	// result.Failures, and returns true — signalling the caller to continue to
86	// the next loop iteration rather than returning.
87	//
88	// If ctx is already cancelled, it returns false so the caller can propagate
89	// the cancellation immediately.
90	func (w *ExecuteBeadWorker) handleOutcomeStoreError(ctx context.Context, beadID, operation string, storeErr error, assignee string, result *ExecuteBeadLoopResult, runtime ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime, now func() time.Time) bool {
91		if ctx.Err() != nil {
92			return false
93		}
94		if runtime.Log != nil {
95			_, _ = fmt.Fprintf(runtime.Log, "outcome store error (%s %s): %v (continuing)\n", operation, beadID, storeErr)
96		}
97		_ = w.Store.AppendEvent(beadID, bead.BeadEvent{
98			Kind:      "loop-error",
99			Summary:   operation + " failed",
100			Body:      storeErr.Error(),
101			Actor:     assignee,
102			Source:    "ddx agent execute-loop",
103			CreatedAt: now().UTC(),
104		})
105		cooldownUntil := now().UTC().Add(StoreErrorCooldown)
106		_ = w.Store.SetExecutionCooldown(beadID, cooldownUntil, "loop-error", operation+": "+storeErr.Error())
107		result.Failures++
108		result.LastFailureStatus = "loop-error"
109		return true
110	}
111	
112	// capLoopCooldown clamps a loop-set cooldown duration to MaxLoopCooldown.
113	// The loop uses this for every SetExecutionCooldown call so no automatic
114	// path can silently park a bead for a year.
115	func capLoopCooldown(d time.Duration) time.Duration {
116		if d <= 0 {
117			return 0
118		}
119		if d > MaxLoopCooldown {
120			return MaxLoopCooldown
121		}
122		return d
123	}

370	func (w *ExecuteBeadWorker) Run(ctx context.Context, rcfg config.ResolvedConfig, runtime ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime) (*ExecuteBeadLoopResult, error) {
371		if w.Store == nil {
372			return nil, fmt.Errorf("execute-bead loop: store is required")
373		}
374		if w.Executor == nil {
375			return nil, fmt.Errorf("execute-bead loop: executor is required")
376		}
377	
378		now := w.Now
379		if now == nil {
380			now = time.Now
381		}
382		assignee := rcfg.Assignee()
383		if assignee == "" {
384			assignee = "ddx"
385		}
386		noProgressCooldown := rcfg.NoProgressCooldown()
387		if noProgressCooldown <= 0 {
388			noProgressCooldown = 6 * time.Hour
389		}
390		maxNoChangesBeforeClose := rcfg.MaxNoChangesBeforeClose()
391		if maxNoChangesBeforeClose <= 0 {
392			maxNoChangesBeforeClose = 3
393		}
394		heartbeatInterval := rcfg.HeartbeatInterval()
395		if heartbeatInterval <= 0 {
396			heartbeatInterval = bead.HeartbeatInterval
397		}
398		harness := rcfg.Harness()
399		model := rcfg.Model()
400		profile := rcfg.Profile()
401	
402		result := &ExecuteBeadLoopResult{}
403		attempted := make(map[string]struct{})
404		// triagedWarned suppresses the one-time boot warning when ComplexityGate is
405		// nil. One warning per Run() invocation is enough; the bool resets on the
406		// next worker boot.
407		triagedWarned := false
408		// hookFailed tracks beads whose pre-claim hook failed on first presentation
409		// in this run. A bead in hookFailed but not attempted gets one retry: on the
410		// second hook failure it moves to attempted so nextCandidate will skip it and
411		// the loop can exit gracefully. This prevents infinite spinning when the hook
412		// always fails while still allowing transient hook failures to be retried.
413		hookFailed := make(map[string]struct{})
414	
415		emit := func(eventType string, data map[string]any) {
416			writeLoopEvent(runtime.EventSink, runtime.SessionID, eventType, data, now().UTC())
417		}
418	
419		emit("loop.start", map[string]any{
420			"worker_id":    runtime.WorkerID,
421			"project_root": runtime.ProjectRoot,
422			"harness":      harness,
423			"model":        model,
424			"session_id":   runtime.SessionID,
425			"assignee":     assignee,
426			"once":         runtime.Once,
427		})
428		// exitReason is populated as the loop exits to surface a structured reason
429		// in the loop.end event (ddx-dc157075 AC #4). Recognized values: "sigterm",
430		// "sigint", "fatal_config", "once_complete", "explicit_poll_zero". The
431		// "providers_exhausted" slot is reserved for ddx-aede917d (quota pause).
432		exitReason := ""
433		defer func() {
434			emit("loop.end", map[string]any{
435				"attempts":            result.Attempts,
436				"successes":           result.Successes,
437				"failures":            result.Failures,
438				"last_failure_status": result.LastFailureStatus,
439				"exit_reason":         exitReason,
440			})
441		}()
442	
443		for {
444			// Respect context cancellation between iterations. Without this check,
445			// a Stop() request (which cancels ctx) would only take effect during
446			// the idle poll sleep — the loop would happily claim the next ready
447			// bead as soon as the current Execute returned, ignoring the cancel.
448			if err := ctx.Err(); err != nil {
449				if exitReason == "" {
450					switch err {
451					case context.Canceled:
452						exitReason = "sigint"
453					case context.DeadlineExceeded:
454						exitReason = "sigterm"
455					default:
456						exitReason = "context_cancelled"
457					}
458				}
459				return result, err
460			}
461	
462			candidate, skips, ok, err := w.nextCandidate(attempted, runtime.LabelFilter)
463			if err != nil {
464				exitReason = "fatal_config"
465				return result, err
466			}
467			// Diagnostic: when nextCandidate selected a lower-priority bead while
468			// at least one higher-priority bead was skipped, emit a structured
469			// picker.priority_skip event naming each skipped bead and the reason
470			// (ddx-9d55601f AC #4). Without this surface, an operator who sees a
471			// worker claim a P2 while P0s sit in `ddx bead ready` cannot tell
472			// whether the picker has a bug, the P0s are filtered upstream, or a
473			// label filter is in play.
474			if ok {
475				emitPickerPrioritySkips(emit, candidate, skips)
476			}
477			if !ok {
478				if result.Attempts == 0 {
479					result.NoReadyWork = true
480					if diag, ok := w.Store.(readyDiagnoser); ok {
481						if breakdown, bErr := diag.ReadyExecutionBreakdown(); bErr == nil {
482							result.NoReadyWorkDetail = NoReadyWorkBreakdown{
483								SkippedEpics:       breakdown.SkippedEpics,
484								SkippedOnCooldown:  breakdown.SkippedOnCooldown,
485								SkippedNotEligible: breakdown.SkippedNotEligible,
486								SkippedSuperseded:  breakdown.SkippedSuperseded,
487								NextRetryAfter:     breakdown.NextRetryAfter,
488							}
489						}
490					}
491				}
492				if runtime.PollInterval <= 0 {
493					// Legacy "drain-and-exit" semantics: operator either passed
494					// --once (handled at end-of-iteration) or explicitly set
495					// --poll-interval=0. ddx-dc157075: with default --poll-interval
496					// flipped to 30s in CLI/server, this path now only fires for
497					// the explicit opt-out case.
498					if runtime.Once {
499						exitReason = "once_complete"
500					} else {
